raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-commitment to interrupt polarized thinking by seeking nuance",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "all-or-nothing frames are recognizable in the moment",
    "partial truth typically exists on both sides of such frames",
    "actively searching for that partial truth is both possible and preferable"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; the antecedent condition (all-or-nothing frame) is paired with a targeted counter-behavior that directly addresses the identified limitation",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the speaker must reliably detect the all-or-nothing state and possess sufficient psychological safety and skill to examine both sides without immediate defensive rejection",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement functions as performative nuance that conceals an unwillingness to ever reach a firm conclusion or that treats all positions as equally partial regardless of evidence"
}
